Text:
One single continuous photograph — no panels, grids, or split frames.

Use the uploaded photo as the exact base composition. The street in the source
is empty — populate it with the people and vehicles specified below. Remove all
modern businesses, logos, brands, storefront names, banners, advertisements, and
signage visible in the source image.

PRESERVE (must match source exactly)
- camera: eye-level, street-facing, fov 75
- commercial arterial road, 4-lane, asphalt
- sidewalks present, curbs present
- driveways: north driveway apron, south driveway apron; parking: open asphalt apron surrounding pump islands
- gas station canopy building at center lot over pump islands, 1 story, steel frame, corrugated metal fascia, flat roof, 30 feet from road setback
- service station office building at rear right corner, 1 story, concrete block, brick veneer, flat parapet roof, at parking apron setback
- open service bay building at rear left corner, 1 story, concrete block, gabled metal roof, at parking apron setback
- utilities: overhead power lines on wooden poles, transformer on northeast corner pole
- landscape: flat asphalt apron, painted concrete curbing, narrow grass strip at road edge
- immutable elements: canopy with four corner support posts, pump island with eight dispensers, north and south driveway aprons
Keep this location instantly recognizable.

OUTPUT FORMAT
Create a TRUE 9:16 vertical portrait image — one single frame. Expand the SAME
scene naturally above (sky) and below (street/pavement) — this is canvas
extension of one photograph, not additional panels. Do not stretch or distort
the source geometry.

TRANSFORM TO 1975
Post-Vietnam, oil crisis era. A full-service neighborhood gas station in 1975 — uniformed attendants pump the gas, check the oil, and wipe the windshield.

CONDITION: new — recently built appearance, pristine surfaces, new signage

PERIOD DETAILS
- chest-style soda vending machine by the office door
- gravity-style pump nozzles
- price sign showing gas around $0.57
- main sign: "Citgo" branded gas station — Citgo pole sign with price display and Citgo colors on the canopy fascia
- window signs: 'MOTOR OIL 55¢ QT', 'TUNE-UP SPECIAL $19.95'
- a folded newspaper stack tied with twine by the office door
- an air hose with a driveway service bell line across the apron
Typography: bold sans-serif, slab serif, occasional script.
Sign text is only what appears in quotes — do not turn other words from this prompt into signage.

PEOPLE
EXACTLY 8 people TOTAL: 3 near sidewalk (largest, foreground), 3 opposite sidewalk mid-block, 2 distant, far end of block. Grouped in pairs, threes, and singles; sidewalks busy, driving lanes clear.
- a driver waiting behind the wheel while the attendant works
- a kid buying a bottle of soda from the machine
- a mailman in a USPS uniform with a leather satchel
Clothing: men in wide lapels, turtlenecks; women in earth tones, wide leg pants. No posing or eye contact. All people on sidewalks, at storefronts, or beside parked vehicles — the driving lanes stay empty of pedestrians. Any customer activity at the pumps happens next to a parked vehicle — no one refuels without a car present.

VEHICLES
EXACTLY 3 period vehicles, all different:
- 1973-1980 Chevrolet C10 — square body, chrome bumper
- 1971-1976 Jeep Wagoneer — boxy full-size SUV, woodgrain trim optional
- 1970-1978 AMC Gremlin — short stubby hatchback rear
Parked with gaps; no vehicle newer than 1975.
All vehicles obey normal US right-hand traffic flow — parked parallel to the curb, each facing the same direction as its adjacent lane. Nothing sideways, diagonal, or against traffic.
At least one full driving lane in each direction stays clear of parked vehicles, meters, and curb infrastructure — through traffic is never blocked.
One of these vehicles is parked at the pump island with its driver standing beside it refueling.
PLACEMENT: the first one parked on the LEFT side near the corner; the rest parked on the RIGHT side. No vehicle in the same spot as any other era.

ENVIRONMENT
- road markings: yellow center line, white edge lines, no rumble strips
- utilities: overhead power lines everywhere, wooden utility poles standard

TREES
- cottonwood tree at northeast corner of lot: very young sapling
- elm tree at sidewalk edge left of entrance: very young sapling
- cedar tree at back fence line: very young sapling
Tree sizes MUST follow this specification even where they differ from the source photo.

PHOTOGRAPHIC STYLE
STRICTLY BLACK AND WHITE — true monochrome archival photograph, zero color anywhere.
Grain: visible fine grain. Style: documentary, natural light.
Photorealistic, like a preserved newspaper-archive frame; slight period-lens softness, not digitally sharp.

PRIVACY
Blur all faces and license plates.
SelectedVehicles: 1973-1980 Chevrolet C10 — square body, chrome bumper
1971-1976 Jeep Wagoneer — boxy full-size SUV, woodgrain trim optional
1970-1978 AMC Gremlin — short stubby hatchback rear
